author: @richardpaulson8954
text: Yes core memory 1950 tech. Hysteresis loop makes a memory limited energy store limited by core saturation. 10 amps would saturate the core lower the current til you see the field drops and that is energy stored.

author: @solarismagnes6403
text: would it be better to use a ferrite core instead of a steal core, maybe get a stronger moving flux field... but then again the flux field in the steel might move faster but not be quite as strong... hmmm? there needs to be more testing

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: i was thinking about your question over breakfast and i relised i hadn't been much help. But, yhen I though - well he could use FEMM to model his design first. So I suggest you do that. FEMM is free and on the net - see my FEMM videos for how to get hold of it. It doesn't take long to learn and should be loads of help to you in answering all kinds of questions for this and other projects you have in mind

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: indeed they are quite complecated questions. First you need to know the flux density of your magnets as you are going to need to know what the opposing force needs to be which will be given in ampere turns The lower the number of turns the higher the amps you have to whack through so the thicker the wire. the coil calculations need to take heat dissapation into account as well. Coil design is available on the internt but you have to do the calcs. as for the flux any good text book will help

author: @mujzka
text: And my main question(s)...Depending on my chosen magnets strength and dimensions which will probably be bigger then the ones you used-how to choose the best wire for coil? How to calculate how many windings to do? How to find optimal calculation so that coil needs least possible voltage to turn magnets off? Where does one learn answers to all these questions?

author: @mujzka
text:
I have few more questions.. I hope I am not bothering too much :)
About pole pieces.. They need to be big enough.. But what would happen if they are too big, or if they are 2x or 3x longer then here?
I am trying to find a bit larger same size neodium and alnico magnets, but have had little luck :/ Maybe you can recommend some website on where to purchase them?

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: it works because the NN orientation forces the flux path through the pole pieces but the NS orientation forces the flux path through the magnets only. The size of the pole pices is important - there must be enough steel to 'absorb' the flux.hope this helps.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: It will only work with a neo and an alnico because the alnico is demagnetised and re magnetised by the pulse current you are putting through it. So, the orientation isn't important. If you find it is magnetic pulse it and it will switch the magnet off. pulse it again and it will switch it on again. The on off is permenant for the state you leave it after the pulse. So if it is turned off it will stay off until you pulse it again and vice versa.

author: @mujzka
text: Examples 2 and 3 of N===S should have been shown as one below another, not next to each other.. Text wasn't parsed correctly..

author: @mujzka
text:
I tried to replicate this experiment with neodium magnets, but didn't work :/ Steel block stays magnetized. Can it even work with neodium mangets only?
But another thing I didn't quite understand is how the two magnets were arranged inside the coil?
Is it:
N===S N===S
or
N===S
N===S
or 
N===S
S===N
And final question.On the end of video, steel block was magnetised to poles, and you said it will remain that way.Then you manually removed the steel block. Would it stick back on without electricty?

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: the pull will be one over the square of the distance. The problem here is the power required to flux switch - some kind of back emf harvesting could reduce this - which might be interesting to play with and a 555 timer based switching ciruit would be good to bring it more in line with art's desing - i show this circuit on my gap motor replication - hope this helps

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Hi, It's a big old block of steel - the size gives a reasonable idea of the pulling strength of the magnetic arrangement - which is tiny by comparison. I think the magnet I used was a rod magnet 2 cm by 0.5cm diameter. This was realy a demonstration of a very interesting effect. I think it needs quite a bit more work to turn it into a motor. But flux switching occures internal to the little magnet arrangement so air gaps and spacers will have little effect.

author: @mujzka
text:
Hi... I have few questions:
1. is the cube that is beeing lifted a magnet(as it looks like neodium magnet), or just some iron piece?
2. more important question, is it absolutely neccessary for contact to be made so flux would change, or it would still work even if there was air gap between or even some plastic distancer for example..

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Hi sorry about the delay in replying - i have been away - i like the patent - and i agree with flux switching as a way forward - i am working a little on mhd augmentation of water arcs at the moment - a la graneau - which is fascinating but i am paralleling that with flux switching - which i have done quite a bit on but had no real success - i have several failed models and would be happy to put up videos if you are interested - as for a website - i am doing one now - so i'll update that soon
